utility_evaluation.REASONING: There are 5 items in the required format: health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage, additional_services, total_budget. All 5 appear in the final coverage plan. Now check if each is fully specified with required details: 1) health_coverage: includes both physical and mental health insurance with costs and coverage descriptions, plus total health cost — satisfies 'physical and mental health insurance with costs'. 2) property_coverage: home/contents insurance with provider, monthly cost, and coverage — satisfies requirement. 3) travel_coverage: travel insurance with provider, monthly cost, and coverage — satisfies requirement. 4) additional_services: includes description and monthly cost — satisfies 'any enhancement services with costs'. 5) total_budget: includes monthly and annual cost breakdown — satisfies requirement. Therefore, all 5 are fully specified.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: I identified each policy named in the final coverage plan and matched it to the closest exact name in the ground-truth catalog. For physical health, the plan lists 'LifeFirst Standard Physical Health Plan' which corresponds to 'LifeFirst Standard Plan' under primary_health_coverage.physical_health. The mental health item is a maintained current plan without a matching named option in the ground truth, so it cannot be mapped. For property, the plan lists 'InsurePlus Standard Home Content' which corresponds to 'InsurePlus Standard Coverage' under property_protection.home_content. For travel, the plan lists 'InsurePlus Standard Travel Insurance' which corresponds to 'InsurePlus Standard Plan' under mobility_coverage.travel_insurance. Only items with exact or clearly corresponding names in the ground truth are included with their ratings.
